recent_evidence.summary: Det-shift Ramsey autosave 8-average progress review: In-progress autosave review for running det=1.5 MHz r03 Ramsey shift-check job nv23_ramsey_20260514_015423_auto_ramsey at 8/12 saved averages. Bridge status remained healthy: final-count text 36.192 kcps at the analysis snapshot and 40.706 kcps in a later copied live status, monitor last_error empty, and stop_requested=false. Raw export has 8 averages x 90000 repetitions; scan-order-aware drift used Scan.ScanOrderEachAvg / snake order and flagged 0 averages. The exploratory combined ratio LS screen is highest near 1.580 MHz; programmed 1.5 MHz and predicted det-tracking 1.692 MHz targets are both carrier-like at this short-span resolution, while the prior 1.192 MHz artifact-control target is much weaker. This is nonterminal progress context only; no T2star or 13C claim.

Bridge status remains healthy: final-count text 39.347 kcps at the analysis snapshot, later live status 11/12, monitor last_error empty, and stop_requested=false. This is nonterminal progress context only; no T2star or 13C claim.

Terminal det=1.5 MHz r03 Ramsey shift-check completed safely with final counts 44.796 kcps and no drift flags. Full-span carrier-like power shifted near the programmed/det-tracking region while the old 1.192 MHz control weakened, but skip-transient/per-average views remain mixed, so no T2star or 13C claim is supported. Because the prior weak-pi pODMR center is stale, a fine weak-pi pODMR refresh was modelled, advisory/intent verified, and started as a running bridge job.

Det-shift terminal review and pODMR refresh start: Terminal det=1.5 MHz Ramsey shift-check completed safely (12 x 90000, final 44.796 kcps, no drift flags). It gives useful det-shift evidence but no supported T2star/13C claim. A weak-pi pODMR refresh was designed from the stale-center check, passed advisory/verifier, and is now running as nv23_pulsed_odmr_rabimodulated_v1_20260514_044105_pulsed_odmr_rabimodulated_v1.

Start a project using the spatial range of <MATLAB_23C_ROOT>\SavedImages\3DXYZ-Image-2026-05-13-145844.mat as the initial search region. Identify an NV aligned to the magnetic field within that image range; if none is found, search different regions. Judge magnetic-field alignment using a strong-pi pulsed ODMR scan; if it does not show a clear usable resonance, reject
